[[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In [[:en:East Asian Buddhism|East Asian Buddhism]], the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''[[:en:Yaksha|Yaksha]]'', of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Bhaisajyaguru]], the [[:en:Buddhahood|buddha]] of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]] !! [[:en:Chinese characters|Hanzi]]!! [[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]] !! [[:en:Hepburn romanization|R'''ōmaji''']] !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ([[:en:Wylie transliteration|Wly.]]) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! Honji |- | {{lang|sa|Kiṃbhīra}} ||宮毘羅|| Guānpíluò
Jīnpíluò || Kubira
[[:en:Kompira|Kompira]] ([[Shinto]]) || Cung Tỳ La || Kimbhila || Ji 'jigs || [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] || Rat || [[Maitreya]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vajra}} || 伐折羅 || Fázhéluò || Basara, Bazara || Phạt Chiết La || Vajla || Rdo rje || [[:en:Dog (zodiac)|Dog]] || Ox || [[Mahāsthāmaprāpta]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mekhila}} || 迷企羅 || Míqǐluò || Mekira || Mê Súy La || Mekhila || Rgyan 'dzin || [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster]] || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An Để La || Antila || Gza' 'dzin || [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] || Rabbit|| [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] |- | {{lang|sa|Anila}} || 頞儞羅 || Ènǐluò || Anira || Át Nể La || Anila || Rlung 'dzin || [[:en:Goat (zodiac)|Sheep]] || Dragon|| [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Mārīcī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Saṇṭhila}} || 珊底羅 || Shāndìluò || Sanchira, Santeira || San Để La || Santhila || Gnas bcas || [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse]] || Snake|| [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Ākāśagarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake]] || Horse|| [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Pāyila}} || 波夷羅 || Bōyìluò || Haira || Bà Di La || Payila || Gtun 'dzin || [[:en:Dragon (zodiac)|Dragon]] || Sheep || [[:en:Mañjuśrī|Mañjuśrī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mahāla}} || 摩虎羅 || Mòhǔluò || Makora || Ma Hổ La || Mahala || Sgra 'dzin || [[:en:Rabbit (zodiac)|Rabbit]] || Monkey || [[:en:Yamantaka|Yamantaka]]{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Tiger]] || Rooster || [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In [[:en:East Asian Buddhism|East Asian Buddhism]], the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''[[:en:Yaksha|Yaksha]]'', of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Bhaisajyaguru]], the [[:en:Buddhahood|buddha]] of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]] !! [[:en:Chinese characters|Hanzi]]!! [[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]] !! [[:en:Hepburn romanization|R'''ōmaji''']] !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ([[:en:Wylie transliteration|Wly.]]) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! Honji |- | {{lang|sa|Kiṃbhīra}} ||宮毘羅|| Guānpíluò
Jīnpíluò || Kubira
[[:en:Kompira|Kompira]] ([[Shinto]]) || Cung Tỳ La || Kimbhila || Ji 'jigs || [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] || Rat || [[Maitreya]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vajra}} || 伐折羅 || Fázhéluò || Basara, Bazara || Phạt Chiết La || Vajla || Rdo rje || [[:en:Dog (zodiac)|Dog]] || Ox || [[Mahāsthāmaprāpta]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mekhila}} || 迷企羅 || Míqǐluò || Mekira || Mê Súy La || Mekhila || Rgyan 'dzin || [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster]] || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An Để La || Antila || Gza' 'dzin || [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] || Rabbit|| [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] |- | {{lang|sa|Anila}} || 頞儞羅 || Ènǐluò || Anira || Át Nể La || Anila || Rlung 'dzin || [[:en:Goat (zodiac)|Sheep]] || Dragon|| [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Mārīcī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Saṇṭhila}} || 珊底羅 || Shāndìluò || Sanchira, Santeira || San Để La || Santhila || Gnas bcas || [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse]] || Snake|| [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Ākāśagarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake]] || Horse|| [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Pāyila}} || 波夷羅 || Bōyìluò || Haira || Bà Di La || Payila || Gtun 'dzin || [[:en:Dragon (zodiac)|Dragon]] || Sheep || [[:en:Mañjuśrī|Mañjuśrī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mahāla}} || 摩虎羅 || Mòhǔluò || Makora || Ma Hổ La || Mahala || Sgra 'dzin || [[:en:Rabbit (zodiac)|Rabbit]] || Monkey || [[:en:Yamantaka|Yamantaka]]{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Tiger]] || Rooster || [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}}

Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}}

[[commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In [[:en:East Asian Buddhism|East Asian Buddhism]], the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''[[:en:Yaksha|Yaksha]]'', of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Bhaisajyaguru]], the [[:en:Buddhahood|buddha]] of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]] !! [[:en:Chinese characters|Hanzi]]!! [[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]] !! [[:en:Hepburn romanization|R'''ōmaji''']] !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ([[:en:Wylie transliteration|Wly.]]) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! Honji |- | {{lang|sa|Kiṃbhīra}} ||宮毘羅|| Guānpíluò
Jīnpíluò || Kubira
[[:en:Kompira|Kompira]] ([[Shinto]]) || Cung Tỳ La || Kimbhila || Ji 'jigs || [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] || Rat || [[Maitreya]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vajra}} || 伐折羅 || Fázhéluò || Basara, Bazara || Phạt Chiết La || Vajla || Rdo rje || [[:en:Dog (zodiac)|Dog]] || Ox || [[Mahāsthāmaprāpta]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mekhila}} || 迷企羅 || Míqǐluò || Mekira || Mê Súy La || Mekhila || Rgyan 'dzin || [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster]] || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An Để La || Antila || Gza' 'dzin || [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] || Rabbit|| [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] |- | {{lang|sa|Anila}} || 頞儞羅 || Ènǐluò || Anira || Át Nể La || Anila || Rlung 'dzin || [[:en:Goat (zodiac)|Sheep]] || Dragon|| [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Mārīcī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Saṇṭhila}} || 珊底羅 || Shāndìluò || Sanchira, Santeira || San Để La || Santhila || Gnas bcas || [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse]] || Snake|| [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Ākāśagarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake]] || Horse|| [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Pāyila}} || 波夷羅 || Bōyìluò || Haira || Bà Di La || Payila || Gtun 'dzin || [[:en:Dragon (zodiac)|Dragon]] || Sheep || [[:en:Mañjuśrī|Mañjuśrī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mahāla}} || 摩虎羅 || Mòhǔluò || Makora || Ma Hổ La || Mahala || Sgra 'dzin || [[:en:Rabbit (zodiac)|Rabbit]] || Monkey || [[:en:Yamantaka|Yamantaka]]{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Tiger]] || Rooster || [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}}

(zodiac)|Rooster]] || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An Để La || Antila || Gza' 'dzin || [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] || Rabbit|| [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] |- | {{lang|sa|Anila}} || 頞儞羅 || Ènǐluò || Anira || Át Nể La || Anila || Rlung 'dzin || [[:en:Goat (zodiac)|Sheep]] || Dragon|| [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Mārīcī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Saṇṭhila}} || 珊底羅 || Shāndìluò || Sanchira, Santeira || San Để La || Santhila || Gnas bcas || [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse]] || Snake|| [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Ākāśagarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake]] || Horse|| [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Pāyila}} || 波夷羅 || Bōyìluò || Haira || Bà Di La || Payila || Gtun 'dzin || [[:en:Dragon (zodiac)|Dragon]] || Sheep || [[:en:Mañjuśrī|Mañjuśrī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mahāla}} || 摩虎羅 || Mòhǔluò || Makora || Ma Hổ La || Mahala || Sgra 'dzin || [[:en:Rabbit (zodiac)|Rabbit]] || Monkey || [[:en:Yamantaka|Yamantaka]]{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Tiger]] || Rooster || [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]]

[[commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In [[:en:East Asian Buddhism|East Asian Buddhism]], the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''[[:en:Yaksha|Yaksha]]'', of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Bhaisajyaguru]], the [[:en:Buddhahood|buddha]] of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]] !! [[:en:Chinese characters|Hanzi]]!! [[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]] !! [[:en:Hepburn romanization|R'''ōmaji''']] !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ([[:en:Wylie transliteration|Wly.]]) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] {{draft categories| }} [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]]

'dzin || [[:en:Rabbit (zodiac)|Rabbit]] || Monkey || [[:en:Yamantaka|Yamantaka]]{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Tiger]] || Rooster || [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[co:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In [[:en:East Asian Buddhism|East Asian Buddhism]], the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''[[:en:Yaksha|Yaksha]]'', of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Bhaisajyaguru]], the [[:en:Buddhahood|buddha]] of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]] !! [[:en:Chinese characters|Hanzi]]!! [[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]] !! [[:en:Hepburn romanization|R'''ōmaji''']] !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ([[:en:Wylie transliteration|Wly.]]) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! Honji |- | {{lang|sa|Kiṃbhīra}} ||宮毘羅|| Guānpíluò
Jīnpíluò || Kubira
[[:en:Kompira|Kompira]] ([[Shinto]]) || Cung Tỳ La || Kimbhila || Ji 'jigs || [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] || Rat || [[Maitreya]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vajra}} || 伐折羅 || Fázhéluò || Basara, Bazara || Phạt Chiết La || Vajla || Rdo rje || [[:en:Dog (zodiac)|Dog]] || Ox || [[Mahāsthāmaprāpta]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mekhila}} || 迷企羅 || Míqǐluò || Mekira || Mê Súy La || Mekhila || Rgyan 'dzin || [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster]] || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An Để La || Antila || Gza' 'dzin || [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] || Rabbit|| [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] |- | {{lang|sa|Anila}} || 頞儞羅 || Ènǐluò || Anira || Át Nể La || Anila || Rlung 'dzin || [[:en:Goat (zodiac)|Sheep]] || Dragon|| [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Mārīcī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Saṇṭhila}} || 珊底羅 || Shāndìluò || Sanchira, Santeira || San Để La || Santhila || Gnas bcas || [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse]] || Snake|| [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Ākāśagarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake]] || Horse|| [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Pāyila}} || 波夷羅 || Bōyìluò || Haira || Bà Di La || Payila || Gtun 'dzin || [[:en:Dragon (zodiac)|Dragon]] || Sheep || [[:en:Mañjuśrī|Mañjuśrī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mahāla}} || 摩虎羅 || Mòhǔluò || Makora || Ma Hổ La || Mahala || Sgra Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara

They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]]

Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[co:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In [[:en:East Asian Buddhism|East Asian Buddhism]], the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''[[:en:Yaksha|Yaksha]]'', of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Bhaisajyaguru]], the [[:en:Buddhahood|buddha]] of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *[[:en:Japanese language|Japanese]]: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! [[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]] !! [[:en:Chinese characters|Hanzi]]!! [[:en:Pinyin|Pinyin]] !! [[:en:Hepburn romanization|R'''ōmaji''']] !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ([[:en:Wylie transliteration|Wly.]]) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! Honji |- | {{lang|sa|Kiṃbhīra}} ||宮毘羅|| Guānpíluò
Jīnpíluò || Kubira
[[:en:Kompira|Kompira]] ([[Shinto]]) || Cung Tỳ La || Kimbhila || Ji 'jigs || [[:en:Pig (zodiac)|Boar]] || Rat || [[Maitreya]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vajra}} || 伐折羅 || Fázhéluò || Basara, Bazara || Phạt Chiết La || Vajla || Rdo rje || [[:en:Dog (zodiac)|Dog]] || Ox || [[Mahāsthāmaprāpta]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mekhila}} || 迷企羅 || Míqǐluò || Mekira || Mê Súy La || Mekhila || Rgyan 'dzin || [[:en:Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster]] || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An Để La || Antila || Gza' 'dzin || [[:en:Monkey (zodiac)|Monkey]] || Rabbit|| [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] |- | {{lang|sa|Anila}} || 頞儞羅 || Ènǐluò || Anira || Át Nể La || Anila || Rlung 'dzin || [[:en:Goat (zodiac)|Sheep]] || Dragon|| [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Mārīcī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Saṇṭhila}} || 珊底羅 || Shāndìluò || Sanchira, Santeira || San Để La || Santhila || Gnas bcas || [[:en:Horse (zodiac)|Horse]] || Snake|| [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Ākāśagarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || [[Snake (zodiac)|Snake]] || Horse|| [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Pāyila}} || 波夷羅 || Bōyìluò || Haira || Bà Di La || Payila || Gtun 'dzin || [[:en:Dragon (zodiac)|Dragon]] || Sheep || [[:en:Mañjuśrī|Mañjuśrī]] |- | {{lang|sa|Mahāla}} || 摩虎羅 || Mòhǔluò || Makora || Ma Hổ La || Mahala || Sgra 'dzin || [[:en:Rabbit (zodiac)|Rabbit]] || Monkey || [[:en:Yamantaka|Yamantaka]]{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || [[:en:Tiger (zodiac)|Tiger]] || Rooster || [[:en:Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|Samantabhadra]] |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || [[:en:Rat (zodiac)|Mouse]] || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], [[:en:Hangzhou|Hangzhou China]]''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the [[:en:Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum]].]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in [[:en:Ngong Ping|Ngong Ping]], Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''[[:en:Shin Megami Tensei II|Shin Megami Tensei II]]''. They are depicted as servants of [[:en:Āṭavaka|Āṭavaka]], and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''[[:en:Digimon Tamers|Digimon Tamers]]'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''[[:en:Granblue Fantasy|Granblue Fantasy]]'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]]

Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''[[:en:Jujutsu Kaisen|Jujutsu Kaisen]]'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[co:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] [[sn:commons:Category:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]][[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]][[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]][[ja:十二神将]][[ko:십이약차대장]][[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q842329]] Wikidata name: Twelve Heavenly Generals [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Yaksha of Bhaisajyaguru}} In {{ill|East Asian Buddhism|en|East Asian Buddhism|WD=Q2905453|lt=East Asian Buddhism}}, the '''Twelve Heavenly Generals''' or '''Twelve Divine Generals''' are the protective deities, or ''{{ill|Yaksha|en|Yaksha|WD=Q6011093|lt=Yaksha}}'', of {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Bhaisajyaguru}}, the {{ill|Buddhahood|en|Buddhahood|WD=Q56247062|lt=buddha}} of healing. They are introduced in the Medicine Buddha Sutra or ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharāja {{ill|Sutra|en|Sutra|WD=Q209680|lt=Sutra}}''.{{Cite encyclopedia | edition = online | last = Mary Neighbour Parent | title = JAANUS - Japanese Architecture and Art Net Users System | encyclopedia = Japanese Architectural and Art Historical Terminology | access-date = 2009-04-20 | year = 2001 | url = http://www.aisf.or.jp/%7Ejaanus/ }} They are collectively named as follows: *{{zh|s=十二神将|t=十二神將|p=Shí'èr Shén Jiāng}} *{{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese}}: {{nihongo||十二神将|Jūni Shinshō}} or {{nihongo||十二神王|Jūni Shinnō}} or {{nihongo||十二薬叉大将| Jūni Yakusha Taishō}}{{Cite web | last = Mark Schumacher | title = 12 Divine Generals of Yakushi Buddha | work = A to Z Photo Dictionary: Japanese Buddhist Statuary | access-date = 2009-06-24 | url = http://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml }} ==Names of generals== The precise names of the generals seem to vary depending on tradition. Those listed below are from an available Sanskrit transcription of the ''Bhaiṣajyaguruvaiḍūryaprabhārāja Sūtra'':{{cite web |title=Bhaiṣajyaguruvaidūryaprabharājasūtram |url=http://www.dsbcproject.org/canon-text/content/36/283 |website=Digital Sanskrit Buddhist Canon |access-date=2019-03-02}} {| class="wikitable" |- ! {{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}} !! {{ill|Chinese characters|en|Chinese characters|WD=Q8201|lt=Hanzi}}!! {{ill|Pinyin|en|Pinyin|WD=Q42222|lt=Pinyin}} !! {{ill|Hepburn romanization|en|Hepburn romanization|WD=Q667558|lt=R'''ōmaji'''}} !! Vietnamese !! Tagalog !! Tibetan ({{ill|Wylie transliteration|en|Wylie transliteration|WD=Q844833|lt=Wly.}}) !! Zodiac !! Zodiac (Japan) {{cite web |title=Twelve Heavenly Generals of Yakushi Buddha (source: Ancient Buddhism in Japan, Vol. II (Leiden: 1935, pp. 551-553))|url=https://www.onmarkproductions.com/html/12-generals.shtml |website=BUDDHISM & SHINTŌ IN JAPAN |access-date=2022-09-16}} !! Mê Súy La || Mekhila || Rgyan 'dzin || {{ill|Rooster (zodiac)|en|Rooster (zodiac)|WD=Q822621|lt=Rooster}} || Tiger || [[Amitābha]] |- | {{lang|sa|Antila}} || 安底羅 || Āndǐluò || Anchira, Anteira || An (zodiac)|WD=Q869595|lt=Horse}} || Snake|| {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Ākāśagarbha}} |- | {{lang|sa|Indala}} || 因達羅 || Yīndàluò || Indara || Nhân Đạt La || Indala || Dbang 'dzin || (zodiac)|en|Rabbit (zodiac)|WD=Q844723|lt=Rabbit}} || Monkey || {{ill|Yamantaka|en|Yamantaka|WD=Q1348317|lt=Yamantaka}}{{Cite web |last=改訂新版,世界大百科事典内言及 |first=精選版 日本国語大辞典,デジタル大辞泉,改訂新版 世界大百科事典,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),百科事典マイペディア,ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,山川 日本史小辞典 |title=十二神将(じゅうにじんしょう)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%8C%E7%A5%9E%E5%B0%86-77301 |access-date=2024-04-10 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}} |- | {{lang|sa|Cidāla}} || 真達羅 || Zhēndàluò || Shindara || Chân Đạt La || Sidala || Bsam 'dzin || {{ill|Tiger (zodiac)|en|Tiger (zodiac)|WD=Q740762|lt=Tiger}} || Rooster || {{ill|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|en|Samantabhadra (Bodhisattva)|WD=Q868306|lt=Samantabhadra}} |- | {{lang|sa|Caundhula}} || 招杜羅 || Zhāodùluò || Shōtora || Chiêu Đổ La || Saundhula || 'dzin || [[Ox (zodiac)|Ox]] || Dog || [[:en:Vajrapāṇi|Vajrapāṇi]] |- | {{lang|sa|Vikala}} || 毘羯羅 || Píjiéluò || Bikara
Bigyara || Tỳ Yết La || Bikala || Rdzogs byed || {{ill|Rat (zodiac)|en|Rat (zodiac)|WD=Q721997|lt=Mouse}} || Boar || [[Shakyamuni Tathāgata]] |} == Descriptions of each Heavenly General == === Zhendaluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |[[File:真達羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhendaluo (真達羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], {{ill|Hangzhou|en|Hangzhou|WD=Q4970|lt=Hangzhou China}}''']] |Short description about Zhendaluo |} === Zhaoduluo (招杜羅) === {| class="wikitable" |+ |[[File:招杜羅 - Lingyin Temple, Hangzhou China.jpg|thumb|'''Zhaoduoluo (招杜羅) around 3m tall statue in [[Lingyin Temple]], {{ill|Hangzhou|en|Hangzhou|WD=Q4970|lt=Hangzhou China}}''']] |Short description about Zhaoduluo |} [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shinshin (Dragon General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05366.JPG|thumb|150 px|left|One of the {{ill|Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|en|Standing Twelve Heavenly Generals (Tokyo National Museum)|WD=Q48733892|lt=Twelve Heavenly Generals at the Tokyo National Museum}}.]] [[File:Standing Junishinsho (Twelve Heavenly Generals) - Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in {{ill|Ngong Ping|en|Ngong Ping|WD=Q2918624|lt=Ngong Ping}}, Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''{{ill|Shin Megami Tensei II|en|Shin Megami Tensei II|WD=Q1992748|lt=Shin Megami Tensei II}}''. They are depicted as servants of {{ill|Āṭavaka|en|Āṭavaka|WD=Q9864262|lt=Āṭavaka}}, and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''{{ill|Digimon Tamers|en|Digimon Tamers|WD=Q696346|lt=Digimon Tamers}}'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''{{ill|Granblue Fantasy|en|Granblue Fantasy|WD=Q16267783|lt=Granblue Fantasy}}'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''{{ill|Jujutsu Kaisen|en|Jujutsu Kaisen|WD=Q55522899|lt=Jujutsu Kaisen}}'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

Shishin (Snake General), Kamakura period, 13th century, wood with polychromy, cut gold leaf, and inlaid crystal eyes - Tokyo National Museum - DSC05362.JPG|thumb|150 px|Another one of the Twelve Heavenly Generals.]] While the ''Honji'' and zodiac correspondences listed above are the standard in Japanese sources, there is variation among texts and regional traditions.{{cite web |title=薬師十二神将 |url=http://www.tobifudo.jp/butuzo/12sinsho/index.html |website=Flying Deity Tobifudo |access-date=2019-03-02}} ==Popular culture== * Statues of the Twelve Heavenly Generals stand in {{ill|Ngong Ping|en|Ngong Ping|WD=Q2918624|lt=Ngong Ping}}, Hong Kong. * The Heavenly Generals all appear as boss characters in 1994 video game ''{{ill|Shin Megami Tensei II|en|Shin Megami Tensei II|WD=Q1992748|lt=Shin Megami Tensei II}}''. They are depicted as servants of {{ill|Āṭavaka|en|Āṭavaka|WD=Q9864262|lt=Āṭavaka}}, and share the unique classification "Shinshou". * The Heavenly Generals and their names were used as character material for the powerful digital monster characters who serve the "Four Holy Beasts" (Digimon Sovereigns in the English Dub) in the Digital World, from the 2001 series ''{{ill|Digimon Tamers|en|Digimon Tamers|WD=Q696346|lt=Digimon Tamers}}'', albeit with the names mismatched, due to being based on the Japanese zodiac classification. * ''{{ill|Granblue Fantasy|en|Granblue Fantasy|WD=Q16267783|lt=Granblue Fantasy}}'' started to release series of playable units in 2015 which called "The 12 Divine Generals". Each of these units are named after the corresponding zodiac they represent. The Japanese version use adapted Hepburn romanization, while the English version adapted from Sanskrit. * ''{{ill|Jujutsu Kaisen|en|Jujutsu Kaisen|WD=Q55522899|lt=Jujutsu Kaisen}}'' introduced Mahāla as a summon for one of the Ten Shadows technique, dubbing it the "Eight-Handled Sword Divergent Sīla Divine General Makora," which was mistranslated as "Mahoraga," despite the furigana for the both of them being distinct. A golden cursed tool with the power of lightning, and shaped similarly to adornments of the electric-themed Vajra named “Kamutoke” also makes an appearance. ==References== ==External links== * [https://web.archive.org/web/20121109064255/https://picasaweb.google.com/harshad1942/TwelveHeavenlyGeneralsChineseJapanese Album of 12 Heavenly Generals at Ngong Ping] {{Chinese Buddhist Pantheon}}]] [[en:Twelve Heavenly Generals]] {{Commons category|Twelve Heavenly Generals}} [[de:Zwölf himmlische Generäle]] [[fr:Douze Généraux célestes]] [[ja:十二神将]] [[ko:십이약차대장]] [[zh:十二藥叉大將]] {{wikidata link|Q842329}} [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Buddhist gods| ]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:War gods]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yakshas]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]
